--- NEW ADDITIONS ---
[only new facts, decisions, preferences, constraints from chunk]
- The modern remnants of the British Empire include the British Overseas Territories (BOTs) and the Commonwealth realms.
- The BOTs are not part of the United Kingdom but are British possessions under the Crown.
- Most of the BOTs have their own constitutions, elected legislatures, and internal self-government; the UK retains responsibility for defense, foreign affairs, and sometimes immigration or policing.
- The Commonwealth realms are independent nations that share the British monarch as head of state, but they are fully sovereign and have their own parliaments, prime ministers, and constitutions.
- The UK still retains 14 overseas territories and shares a ceremonial monarch with 15 independent realms.
